behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real recursive filesystem watching, not just local parsing logic. MOCK-MISS: this is about watcher-to-pipeline handoff for nested paths. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: in-process fake events can miss real watcher recursion/path behavior, so a real directory tree and OS file events are needed. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text (no AI/LLM step; single daemon pipeline behavior).
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon against a real temp watched directory, create a nested subdirectory, write a file there, and assert exactly one transformed output is produced for that nested file path.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module interaction between request intake, dedupe state/window config, and pipeline dispatch. MOCK-MISS: a pure unit test of the dedupe function can miss handoff bugs (e.g., request path normalization/config wiring). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess in the behavior text, no AI dependency, and no multi-agent sequence, so System/Agentic/Workflow are not required. Rejected higher level with concrete disqualifier: text specifies dedupe of 'processing request' in a time window, not OS event semantics.
behaviors.test_description: In one process, wire intake + dedupe + dispatcher with a controllable clock, submit the same file path twice within the configured window, and assert only one dispatch reaches the transformation pipeline.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly names OS-emitted duplicate filesystem events, so REAL-INFRA applies directly. MOCK-HIDE: synthetic single-event or simplified fake-event tests can pass while real watcher backends emit event bursts. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow disqualified by behavior text (no model decisions; no agent pipeline).
behaviors.test_description: With the daemon running against a real watched directory, perform a file write pattern known to trigger multiple fs events and assert the pipeline runs once and only one output artifact is produced.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is long-running watcher behavior across real directory deletion/recreation lifecycle. REAL-INFRA: requires OS filesystem state transitions; in-process mocks often hide watcher backend re-subscription issues. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow disqualified by behavior text (no AI/LLM, no multi-agent sequence).
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real directory, delete the watched directory, recreate it at the same path, then add a new file and verify it is processed without restarting the daemon process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on real file metadata and arrival timing under watcher/event queue behavior. MOCK-MISS/MOCK-HIDE: mocked timestamps/events may not reproduce filesystem timestamp precision and race ordering failures. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow disqualified by behavior text (no model output dependency; single daemon workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple files nearly simultaneously in the real watched directory with controlled creation times, then assert processing/output order matches the files’ actual creation timestamps.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real write-in-progress timing versus watcher event timing. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes rarely capture partial-write visibility, lock/flush timing, and backend event ordering accurately. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow disqualified by behavior text (no AI/LLM, no multi-agent chain).
behaviors.test_description: Write a large file incrementally (or keep file handle open) in the watched directory while daemon is running, trigger arrival event naturally, and assert processing starts only after write completion and final content is used.
